title: guapira rotundifolia
intro: Guapira rotundifolia, also known as Guapira, is a plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the family Rhamnaceae. Guapira rotundifolia has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Guapira rotundifolia is commonly prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in traditional medicine in the form of tinctures and poultices.
